exemplar.title: How do different types of traditional medicine ingredients, such as turmeric (Curcuma longa), ginger (Zingiber officinale), tamarind (Tamarindus indica), lemongrass (Cymbopogon citratus), and cinnamon (Cinnamomum verum), exhibit antimicrobial activity against the growth of Escherichia coli?

detailedExemplarData.title: How do different types of traditional medicine ingredients, such as turmeric (Curcuma longa), ginger (Zingiber officinale), tamarind (Tamarindus indica), lemongrass (Cymbopogon citratus), and cinnamon (Cinnamomum verum), exhibit antimicrobial activity against the growth of Escherichia coli?

subject.title: Biology
subject.description:
**IB Biology** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **four interconnected themes: Unity and Diversity, Form and Function, Interaction and Interdependence, and Continuity and Change**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, scientific inquiry, data analysis, and practical investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice and data-based questions | 1 hour 20 min | 2 hours | 36% |
| **Paper 2** | Short and extended response questions | 1 hour 30 min | 2 hours 30 min | 44% |
| **Scientific Investigation** | Individual research project | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Research question is situated within a specific, discipline-appropriate context with well-cited justification for testing herbal extracts against E. coli.
Key methodological considerations (variables, risk, ethics, preliminary trials) are clearly explained, demonstrating an understanding of reliable data collection.
The methodology description is sufficiently detailed to allow reproducibility with only minor ambiguities.
weaknesses: Controlled variables table omits critical parameters (disc diameter, extract volume), affecting full methodological clarity.
Some safety protocols (disposal procedures for biohazardous discs) are incomplete in the risk assessment.
The null hypothesis is imprecisely stated and solvent-to-sample ratios are not specified for full reproducibility.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments placed Research Design in the top band overall, with a perfect score for context and strong performance in methodological explanation, but minor omissions in controls and safety detail. This merits a mid-upper mark of 5/6, reflecting excellent contextualization and explanation while acknowledging small gaps preventing a perfect score.

strengths: Data recording and processing are communicated clearly and largely precisely, with well-organized raw and processed tables including means, standard deviations and labeled graphs.
Processing of data (means, SDs, ANOVA, Tukey HSD) is carried out appropriately and accurately, matching reported values.
Qualitative observations and sample calculations reinforce the quality of data processing.
weaknesses: Consideration of uncertainties is present but incomplete: uncertainties are not propagated into mean, SD or statistical tests.
Minor formatting issues (artefacts in PDF, unlabeled error bars) reduce clarity.
No normality or homogeneity checks precede the ANOVA, limiting statistical rigour.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands achieved topâband performance (clear communication and accurate processing) and one strand showed only partial consideration of uncertainties. This profile aligns with a solid band-3-4 performance, warranting 4/6 to recognize strong data handling with room for more rigorous uncertainty treatment.

strengths: The conclusion directly answers the research question and is fully consistent with the statistical analysis presented.
Justification of results is sound, citing the rejection of Hâ and focusing only on extracts that showed inhibition.
Relevant comparisons to accepted scientific context (published inhibition zone values and mechanistic hypotheses) reinforce the validity of the conclusion.
weaknesses: Discussion of causal mechanisms (e.g., how specific bioactives exert antimicrobial effects) is relatively brief and could be expanded.
Depth of contextual analysis could be improved by integrating additional literature on solvent effects or Gram-negative specificity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both conclusion strands sit firmly in the 5â6 band, with a justified, relevant conclusion and appropriate scientific context comparison. Minor depth limitations prevent a top mark, so a 5/6 suitably reflects strong, evidence-based reasoning.

strengths: The report identifies specific methodological weaknesses (contamination, solvent polarity, timing) and explains their relative impact on data reliability.
Proposed improvements (aseptic technique, simultaneous incubation, use of different solvents) are realistic and directly linked to identified limitations.
Evaluation shows clear understanding of how methodological changes would enhance validity and breadth of results.
weaknesses: Explanations of the relative impact of each weakness are clear but lack quantitative estimation of their effects.
Improvements could be further strengthened by predicting expected reductions in error or contamination rates.
Additional discussion of resource or feasibility constraints for proposed changes would enhance critical depth.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Evaluation strands demonstrate specific, explained weaknesses and improvements, meriting placement in band 3â4. While the links between limitations and remedies are well articulated, the absence of quantitative impact analysis precludes a higher mark. Thus, 4/6 accurately reflects a strong but not exhaustive evaluation.

strandSelections.reasoning: The conclusion explicitly answers the research question, cites the statistical rejection of Hâ, and is fully consistent with the data (zones only for tamarind and lemongrass). The justification is sound, but causal mechanisms are touched upon only briefly. This fits solidly in the 5-6 band; a mark of 5 reflects the strong match with evidence while reserving 6 for a more nuanced rationale.

Raw-data tables include units and uncertainty, processed tables show means/SD, and a bar graph with error bars is supplied. Layout is logical and legible, though occasional formatting artefacts appear in the PDF. Overall the recording and processing are **clear (easily followed) and largely precise (appropriate sig. figs, labelled axes)**âmeriting the 5-6 band; a score of 5 recognises minor formatting issues.

Means, standard deviations, one-way ANOVA and Tukey HSD are all correctly executed and interpreted. The calculations shown match values in tables, indicating **appropriate and accurate processing**. A minor limitation is the absence of a normality check, but overall quality clearly meets band 5-6; a 5 rewards accuracy while acknowledging that further statistical rigour could raise it to 6.

Specific weaknesses are identifiedâplate contamination, solvent polarity, timing differencesâand their **relative impact** on reliability is explained (e.g., contamination may confound visible zones). The explanations are clear but not extensively quantified, placing the work securely in band 5-6; a score of 5 is appropriate.

The student compares tamarind and lemongrass inhibition diameters to published values and discusses possible mechanistic alignment (cell lysis, oxidative stress). These references are relevant and integrated, though depth is moderate rather than exhaustive. Hence the work meets the descriptor of a justified conclusion through **relevant comparison** to accepted scienceâearning 5 in the upper band.

The student not only states the research question but embeds it in a well-developed discussion of herbal medicine, specific plant bioactives and the medical relevance of E. coli. Each species is briefly introduced with citations that establish why its antimicrobial potential is worth testing. This situates the question in a **specific, discipline-appropriate context** rather than a broad or generic background, fully matching the 5-6 descriptor.

The student states ruler uncertainty (Â±0.5 mm) and comments on rounding effects, and error bars in the graph represent SD. However, no uncertainty is propagated into means or the ANOVA, and instrument uncertainties for other apparatus are only mentioned qualitatively. This demonstrates a **consideration** of uncertainties but with significant omissions, matching the middle band (3-4); a lower score of 3 is most fitting.

Improvements address each cited weakness (better aseptic technique, simultaneous incubation, use of ethanol/methanol extracts) and include reasoning about how they would enhance validity or range of bioactives. The student **explains** these links rather than merely stating them, satisfying the 5-6 descriptor; again, a 5 reflects solid but not exhaustive elaboration.

Key methodological factors (independent, dependent and controlled variables, risk, ethics, preliminary trial outcomes) are all identified and their relevance to reliable data collection is **explained**âe.g. why 8 trials are used, why 72 h incubation was chosen, how solvent polarity may limit extraction.  Minor gaps remain (no discussion of disc size effect or control discs), so the explanation is strong but not flawless; the upper band is appropriate but a mid-band mark of 5 best reflects those small omissions.

The step-by-step method, materials list and variable table are detailed enough that another student could reproduce the work with only small ambiguities (exact volume of extract placed on each disc, true disc diameter, concentration of extracts after soaking). Because these minor uncertainties persist, the description aligns with âreproducible with few ambiguities,â corresponding to band 3-4; the stronger end of that band (4) is justified by generally high clarity.

annotations.comment: The research question is clearly articulated and situated within a specific context of traditional medicine and E. coli, demonstrating strong alignment with the research question context descriptor.
The background provides a well-developed discussion of herbal medicine practices with relevant citations, effectively contextualising the research question.

Consider strengthening the link between each bioactive mechanism and the experimental design by explicitly describing how curcumin, citral, tannins, etc., will interact with E. coli growth in your setup.
The independent variable description identifies herbal extracts and trial repetitions but omits the exact concentration or mass-to-solvent ratio needed for reproducibility.
Provide explicit details on disc volume and extract application (e.g., ÂµL per disc) to eliminate ambiguities and ensure full reproducibility of the methodology.
The controlled variable table is empty, so critical controls such as disc diameter, volume of extract per disc and agar volume are not documented.
Essential controlled variables such as filter paper disc size and solvent volume per disc are missing from the variables table; include these to fully explain control methods.
The preliminary trial description clearly explains how incubation time adjustments improved consistency, supporting methodological robustness.
The null hypothesis is defined as mean = 0 rather than stating equality of means among treatments; revise Hâ to reflect no significant difference between groups.
The turmeric and ginger extraction protocol is clearly described but lacks a specified solvent-to-sample ratio to ensure consistency across trials.
The experimentation process lists clear, numbered steps that allow reproducibility, including plate preparation and incubation parameters.
Add scale bars or ruler markings to photographic figures to provide a quantitative reference for inhibition zone sizes.
Recording '0' for no inhibition zones is acceptable but may be misinterpreted; consider using 'ND' or 'no inhibition' with a legend for clarity.
The risk assessment table entry for organic turmeric is truncated, omitting prevention and disposal methods; complete this to address safety thoroughly.
Include disposal procedures for biohazardous paper discs in safety considerations to ensure complete coverage of experimental hazards.
Qualitative observations effectively note contamination potential and incorporate literature, demonstrating clear communication of qualitative data.
Sample calculation clearly shows substitution into the mean formula and yields the correct numeric result, supporting data processing quality.
Error bars should be explicitly labelled in the graph legend to show the standard deviations and reinforce precise data communication.
The ANOVA summary table contains all calculations and p-value interpretation, but its complexity could be reduced to highlight key SS, df, MS and F values.
Explicitly state how the Â±0.5 mm measurement uncertainty is propagated through mean and SD calculations to demonstrate precise processing.
The processed data table presents means and SDs with appropriate significant figures and units, reflecting accurate data processing.
Include axis labels indicating error bars represent SD and note sample size on the bar graph to enhance clarity and precision.
The discussion recognises systematic error due to ruler rounding, showing an appropriate consideration of measurement uncertainties.
Perform normality and homogeneity of variance tests before ANOVA to ensure assumptions are met and strengthen the statistical analysis.
The conclusion succinctly answers the research question with justification from statistical results, fully consistent with the analysis.
Literature comparisons of inhibition zones provide relevant scientific context and reinforce the validity of the conclusions drawn.
Evaluation acknowledges low standard deviations and trial number adequacy, demonstrating an understanding of methodological consistency.
Quantify the anticipated reduction in contamination rates by implementing stricter aseptic handling and sealed incubation to strengthen the improvement plan.
Proposing the use of ethanol or methanol extracts demonstrates understanding of solvent polarityâs impact on bioactive compound extraction.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
The research question is stated without context.
Methodological considerations associated with collecting data relevant to the research question are stated.
The description of the methodology for collecting or selecting data lacks the detail to allow for the investigation to be reproduced.
The description of the methodology for collecting or selecting data allows for the investigation to be reproduced with few ambiguities or omissions.
The research question is outlined within a broad context.
Methodological considerations associated with collecting relevant and sufficient data to answer the research question are described.
The research question is described within a specific and appropriate context.
Methodological considerations associated with collecting relevant and sufficient data to answer the research question are explained.
The description of the methodology for collecting or selecting data allows for the investigation to be reproduced.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
The recording and processing of the data is communicated but is neither clear nor precise.
Some processing of data relevant to addressing the research question is carried out but with major omissions, inaccuracies or inconsistencies.
The recording and processing of data shows limited evidence of the consideration of uncertainties.
The processing of data relevant to addressing the research question is carried out but with some significant omissions, inaccuracies or inconsistencies.
The communication of the recording and processing of the data is either clear or precise.
The recording and processing of data shows evidence of a consideration of uncertainties but with some significant omissions or inaccuracies.
The processing of data relevant to addressing the research question is carried out appropriately and accurately.
The recording and processing of data shows evidence of an appropriate consideration of uncertainties.
The communication of the recording and processing of the data is both clear and precise.

rubricCriterionStrands.name: Communication of data recording and processing

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
The conclusion makes superficial comparison to the accepted scientific context.
A conclusion is stated that is relevant to the research question but is not supported by the analysis presented.
A conclusion is described that makes some relevant comparison to the accepted scientific context.
A conclusion is described that is relevant to the research question but is not fully consistent with the analysis presented.
A conclusion is justified that is relevant to the research question and fully consistent with the analysis presented.
A conclusion is justified through relevant comparison to the accepted scientific context.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
Realistic improvements to the investigation are stated.
The report states generic methodological weaknesses or limitations.
Realistic improvements to the investigation that are relevant to the identified weaknesses or limitations, are described.
The report describes specific methodological weaknesses or limitations.
Realistic improvements to the investigation, that are relevant to the identified weaknesses or limitations, are explained.
The report explains the relative impact of specific methodological weaknesses or limitations.
